notes.anchor: If he should tear himself away from us
notes.note: Brutus fears that Lepidus, having just defected to Antony, might survive militarily, in which case his children (Brutus's nephews through his sister Iunia Tertia's marriage to Cassius — but here Lepidus's children via Iunia, Brutus's sister) would be hostages to the enemy's fury; the letter makes clear Brutus acknowledges Lepidus's defection but hopes the Senate will spare his kin.

notes.anchor: I have succeeded to them in their father's place
notes.note: Brutus's sister Iunia (not Tertia/Cassius's wife, but a different sister) was married to Lepidus; Brutus declares himself surrogate paterfamilias for Lepidus's children in order to strengthen his appeal to Cicero for clemency toward them.

notes.anchor: calling to witness our bond of family and your goodwill towards me
notes.note: "Necessitudo" denotes a binding personal tie (here the close political friendship between Brutus and Cicero); Brutus is invoking both the private relationship and his own moral authority as a basis for the request.
"Consularis" (consular) was the rank held by any former consul; Cicero had held the consulship in 63 BC, and the title carried lifelong prestige and a formal role in Senate deliberations.
"Kal. Quintilibus ex castris" = Kalends of Quinctilis (1 July) 43 BC; Brutus was writing from his camp in Macedonia or Thrace, commanding the armies of the eastern republican cause.
